commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c318e70c972e5b32a5a17f4103f846f29d05fcbd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR extracts Claude live `can_use_tool` response construction into a helper and returns the original tool input as `updatedInput` for allowed control responses, with a focused allow/deny unit test.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +44. Total +59 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.6.10 still emit the old allow payload, and the linked issue gives concrete OpenClaw plus Claude Code 2.1.156 ZodError steps. I did not run a live Claude CLI session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the branch has a dependency-contract finding, missing contributor real-behavior proof, and an open sibling cluster; automation cannot supply contributor proof.

target.title: fix(agents): allow empty-error-retry when replayMetadata is absent (#97877)
target.body:
Fixes #97877.

## What Problem This Solves

In `runEmbeddedAgent`, `normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult` calls `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(raw)` which fills `{ hadPotentialSideEffects: true }` when `raw.replayMetadata` is absent. A model call that fails with HTTP 5xx before any tool execution has no `replayMetadata`, but the fallback incorrectly marks it as side-effectful. By the time `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` checks the attempt, the metadata is already filled in — the retry is blocked in production even though no tools ran.

## Why This Change Was Made

Two changes in the production caller path:

1. **`normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult`** (run.ts:421): Preserve `raw.replayMetadata` as-is — no longer fills the fallback. When the model call fails before tool execution, `replayMetadata` stays `undefined`, carrying the absent signal through to retry eligibility.

2. **Prompt failover replay-safety check** (run.ts:3169): Changed `attempt.replayMetadata.replaySafe` to `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(attempt).replaySafe`. This is the only other direct `.replayMetadata` field access in the runner — all other consumers already use `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata()` or `observeReplayMetadata()` which have their own fallback logic.

In `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` (incomplete-turn.ts:548): Changed from `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(params.attempt).hadPotentialSideEffects` to `params.attempt.replayMetadata?.hadPotentialSideEffects`. With the normalization fix, `replayMetadata` can now be `undefined` when the model call failed before tools ran. The optional chaining treats absent metadata as no side effects in this attempt and allows the retry.

## User Impact

Agents recover from transient 5xx provider errors on any turn — regardless of whether prior turns executed tool calls. The documented `[empty-error-retry]` retry of up to 3 attempts now fires when the model call itself fails before producing output or calling tools.

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed**: `normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult` filled the unsafe fallback before retry eligibility ran, making the helper-level fix invisible in production.

**Real environment tested**: Node 24.13.1, local OpenClaw checkout.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
node --import tsx -e "import { shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn, resolveAttemptReplayMetadata } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.js';
// 4 cases: raw absent / normalized fallback / explicit side effects / explicit safe
"
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
1. RAW absent (5xx before tools): PASS retry=YES
2. NORMALIZED fallback (OLD broken path): retry=NO (BLOCKED)
3. Side effects (safety preserved): PASS retry=NO
4. Safe (explicit ok): PASS retry=YES
```

- Case 1: RAW absent metadata → retry ALLOWED (the production fix — would have been blocked before)
- Case 2: Shows the old path was broken — normalization fallback blocked retry
- Case 3: Explicit side effects still block retry (safety preserved)
- Case 4: Explicit replay-safe metadata allows retry

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  22 passed (22)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  111 passed (111)

node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts
(passed, exit 0)
```

**Observed result after fix**: `normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult` preserves `raw.replayMetadata` without filling the fallback. When the raw attempt has no metadata (model 5xx before tool execution), `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` sees `undefined` via optional chaining and allows the retry. The `observeReplayMetadata` call on line 2411 still correctly handles absent metadata for accumulated state (has its own fallback — unchanged). Explicit side-effect metadata still blocks retry. All other metadata consumers (`resolveAttemptReplayMetadata` at other call sites) are unchanged and still get their own fallback.

**What was not tested**: Live E2E with a real OpenRouter-deployed agent hitting an actual 5xx. The fix is verified through the real `runEmbeddedAgent` caller path (normalize → retry check) via focused + broad agent test suites (133 tests).

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts --run` — 22 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts --run` — 111 passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts` — exit 0

## Root Cause

`normalizeEmbeddedRunAttemptResult` at run.ts:421 unconditional calls `resolveAttemptReplayMetadata(raw)` which falls back to `{ hadPotentialSideEffects: true, replaySafe: false }` when `raw.replayMetadata` is absent. A model call that fails with 5xx before producing any content or tool calls has no `replayMetadata` — `buildAttemptReplayMetadata` was never invoked because no tool activity occurred. But the normalization fills the conservative fallback, and by the time `shouldRetrySilentErrorAssistantTurn` checks the attempt, the metadata is always populated. The fix preserves the raw absent signal through normalization, and the retry helper treats absent metadata as no tools ran in this attempt, allow retry.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Model used**: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context)
- **Co-Authored-By**: Already in commit message
